segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The vent shaft was a stone throat that refused to swallow.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik went first, shoulders scraping both walls, muttering cheerfully obscene things at geology. Vex slid after him sideways, breath clipped to keep her ribs from complaining out loud. Serana followed, armor ghosting sparks from old rock.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan brought up the rear, dragging nothing but a long, obedient shadow.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Grit rained in fits. Serana laced a paper-thin ward over their faces -- muscle memory, not miracle -- and it skinned the dust from their eyes when the shaft shed a handful of centuries. The light was hers -- steady gold from trained discipline, not divine gift -- and it held.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Remind me,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik grunted, wedging through a pinch,

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "why I volunteered the route most likely to make me a cork."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Because the alternative involved dying beautifully underground,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said. She tried a small breath; her ribs sparked protest. She hid the wince behind a scowl.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "You also said, and I quote, 'Left shaft has the coldest air -- surface that way.'"

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Present-Thornik applauds Past-Thornik's olfactory heroism,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, pleased despite himself.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They squeezed into a run of broader tunnel, air colder, cleaner. Frost rimed old soot on the stone. Above them: a ribbon of blue like a promise.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana climbed on discipline more than faith. Her goddess had been a static-laced channel for days; the old prayers returned as faint as wishful thinking. Fine. Duty would do. Duty and the ward under her skin, thin as paper and twice as stubborn.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Behind, Durgan stopped once, hand braced to the wall. He wiped his nose with the back of his hand -- checking, confirming -- but the skin came away clean.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not yet, he thought. But soon.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He could feel it building. The intervals between shadow-tests were shortening. What had been hours was now minutes. He dug his nails into his palm until pain returned his hands to him, then climbed on.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: At a three-way split, Thornik sniffed the drafts, checked his grandfather's blood-spattered diagram, and pointed left.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Coldest. And it tastes like snow,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, delighted.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Your nose is a menace,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, grimacing as she tried to keep pace. The ribs pulled with every breath.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "It's also getting us out,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said, gentler than the banter wanted to be.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They climbed another hour, then the shaft widened to a slot of white.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The foursome spilled onto a ledge scraped by old avalanches, the world dropping away in a dignified hurry.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Sky. Actual, indifferent sky.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik shaded his eyes, traced the east face.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "There -- half-hidden by scree. The primary Forge Road exit. If they made it through the forges, they'll come out there."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "How far?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana asked.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Two hours along the band. Maybe less if the goats have been generous."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Then we move,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said. She looked once at the mountain, where the wind carved the ridges into saints and knives, and whispered the two words she hadn't let herself say aloud:

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "For Elowen."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's hand found her dagger hilt, white-knuckled. She said nothing. Didn't need to.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They started the traverse, Durgan last, shadow flat and patient at his heels.
